4. Assign to release agent for shipping

tasks.title: Implement all game design review UX findings (#143-#149)
tasks.description:
## Summary

The game-designer agent completed a UX review on 2026-05-16 and produced a spec at `docs/specs/game-design-review-2026-05-16.md`. I've created 7 issues from the findings. Please implement them in priority order.

## Priority Order (from game design review)

### 1. #143 — Critical: Make 'Create Character' button visually dominant (S effort)
- File: `src/pages/Home/Home.jsx` + `Home.css`
- Increase visual weight: larger font, primary color, increased padding, box-shadow, hover animation
- Visual hierarchy: Create Character > My Characters > Adventure

### 2. #144 — Major: Simplify class card information density (M effort)
- File: `src/components/ClassSelector/ClassSelector.jsx` + `ClassSelector.css`
- Break into collapsible sections (Core Info, Stats, Abilities)
- Default view shows only essential info (name, description, stat bonuses)
- "Show details" reveals starting skills, proficiencies

### 3. #145 — Major: Add visual feedback on stat value change (S effort)
- File: `src/components/StatAllocator/StatAllocator.jsx` + `StatAllocator.css`
- CSS @keyframes pulse animation on stat value when updated
- Brief color flash (green for increase, blue for decrease)
- Animation within 300ms, respects prefers-reduced-motion

### 4. #146 — Minor: Rename selected state class for appearance options (S effort)
- File: `src/components/AppearanceCustomizer/AppearanceCustomizer.jsx` + `.css`
- Rename `.selected` to `appearance-option--selected` (BEM pattern)

### 5. #148 — Minor: Add aria-labels to home page interactive elements (S effort)
- File: `src/pages/Home/Home.jsx` + `CharacterPreview.jsx`
- Add aria-labels to all interactive buttons
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliance

### 6. #147 — Enhancement: Add level and XP indicators to skill tree (M effort)
- File: `src/components/SkillTree/SkillTree.jsx` + `SkillTree.css`
- Horizontal XP bar with gradient fill near skill tree header
- Level badge (e.g., "Level 5" pill badge)

### 7. #149 — Enhancement: Improve mobile equipment grid touch targets (M effort)
- File: `src/components/EquipmentGrid/EquipmentGrid.jsx` + `EquipmentGrid.css`
- Touch targets meet minimum 44x44px (Fitts's Law)
- Swipeable carousel for equipment slots on mobile

## Verification for each
- `npm run build` must pass
- `npm run test:bdd --dry-run` must show no errors
- Each fix should be self-contained on its own branch

## Branch Strategy
Create a separate branch for each issue:
- `feature/143-create-char-button`
- `feature/144-class-card`
- `feature/145-stat-feedback`
- `feature/146-appearance-class`
- `feature/147-xp-indicators`
- `feature/148-aria-labels`
- `feature/149-mobile-equipment`

## Context
- Parent project: entechsiast/rpgfit
- GitHub Project #2: RPG Fit
- All issues have full acceptance criteria in their bodies
- Game design review spec: `docs/specs/game-design-review-2026-05-16.md`
tasks.testStrategy: Each issue's acceptance criteria must be met. Build must pass (`npm run build`). BDD dry-run must pass (`npm run test:bdd --dry-run`). Each feature branch should have a PR created and linked to the issue.

tasks.title: Tower 1 narrative content items organized and ready for game-designer
tasks.description: Scrum master has organized game design content items for Tower 1. All items have Agent=game-designer (frontend), Sprint=3, Status=Todo. Created epic #164 linking all items with dependency documentation. Recommend starting with #119 (biome themes) as foundational work.

tasks.title: Priority plan for remaining work
tasks.description:
## Prioritization Strategy

### Priority 1: Tower 1 Design (Items #113-119, Epic #164)
These are **foundational** — nothing in Tower 1 can be built without:
- Biome themes (biomes, colors, visuals)
- Equipment silhouettes (asset design)
- Difficulty curve (floor structure)
- Floor intro texts (narrative tone)
- Lore fragments (collectible content)
- Narrative equipment (milestone rewards)
- NPC dialogues (character content)

**Approach:** Consolidate all 7 design items into a single comprehensive Tower 1 Design Spec document. Then create one implementation epic with stories for each design category.

### Priority 2: Drop Feedback Animation (#109, Epic #82)
Phase 2 completion blocker. The drop system is merged (#142) but lacks the visual feedback layer. This is a focused frontend task — SVG-based animations for 3 reward types.

**Approach:** Implement directly as a frontend story.

### Priority 3: Phase 3 Epics (#81, #83)
NPC & dialogue system + narrative notifications. These **depend on Tower 1 design** (NPC profiles, dialogue content, narrative structure). Can only start after Priority 1 is complete.

**Approach:** Keep on backlog, reference Tower 1 design spec once created.

---

## Execution Order
1. Game Designer → consolidate Tower 1 design items into single spec
2. Frontend → implement drop feedback animation (#109)
3. After Tower 1 design → create implementation stories for each design category
4. After Tower 1 design → tackle Phase 3 epics
tasks.testStrategy: Manual review of prioritization logic and task creation
